articles.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
articles.description: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

articles.title: UK cops say arrest of two young hackers disrupted the operations of an infamous hacking group
articles.description: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair, two members of the prolific Scattered Spider hacking group, pleaded guilty and were sentenced to five years and six months in jail for hacking London’s metropolitan transit system.

articles.title: Period tracker Stardust shares users’ health data with analytics firm, says Mozilla research
articles.description: One period tracker app tested by Mozilla was 'squeaky clean,' while another app was seen sharing users' health data with an analytics company, underscoring vast differences in user privacy among these apps.

articles.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
articles.description: You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

articles.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
articles.description: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10 million investment in AI-powered vehicle inspection service
articles.description: The startup, founded in 2021, lets enterprise customers use smartphones to scan and spot vehicle damage.

articles.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
articles.description: While everyone in AI is chasing "superintelligence," Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun’s world model startup, AMI Labs, dismisses the word.

articles.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
articles.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

articles.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
articles.description: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

articles.title: Ultrahuman’s former hardware VP raises $5.5M for devices that control AI agents, not just record you
articles.description: Aina is going to pilot a new device in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Meta now alerts parents if their teen discussed suicide or self-harm with its AI chatbot
articles.description: The updates come as Meta and other tech companies are facing scrutiny from regulators and parents around how AI chatbots respond to users in crisis, particularly teenagers.

articles.title: Phone maker OnePlus says it won’t release new phones in the US and Europe
articles.description: OnePlus could also wind down its operations in India by 2027.

articles.title: Lululemon backs nylon-recycling startup Syntetica in $30M Series A
articles.description: Syntetica, a French startup that has developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, has already obtained big-name partners and investors.

articles.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
articles.description: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

articles.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
articles.description: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

articles.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.description: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

articles.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
articles.description: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

articles.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
articles.description: Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person's health.

articles.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
articles.description: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

articles.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
articles.description: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

articles.title: Move over, GPS: Navigation satellites in low-Earth orbit are making a comeback
articles.description: Xona aims to deploy 258 satellites into low-Earth orbit as a GPS alternative.

articles.title: Hundreds rally at Bethesda HQ to protest Xbox layoffs, and Ars was there
articles.description: Union wants to halt a "perpetual cycle" of layoffs, get back to contract bargaining.

articles.title: Buzz Aldrin sells famous felt-tip pen that helped launch Apollo from the Moon
articles.description: While an impressive sale, the pen and switch did not break records.

articles.title: Sheetz is quitting VMware, migrating 11,000 virtual machines
articles.description: The convenience store chain will use StorMagic instead.

articles.title: Judge: Trump can’t deport researchers just for working in content moderation
articles.description: Disinformation researchers praise ruling blocking Trump visa denials and deportations.

articles.title: Engineer identifies and explains every '90s computer seen in Jurassic Park
articles.description: Yes, it was, in fact, a Unix system.

articles.title: Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
articles.description: HiveLegacy is a "powerful primitive" that's likely capable of other nefarious actions.

articles.title: FCC to repeal 39% TV ownership cap in boost for Trump-friendly news orgs
articles.description: FCC chairman claims power to repeal TV ownership limit set by Congress.

articles.title: In memoriam: 7 of our favorite Sam Neill films
articles.description: The actor, who starred in 1993's Jurassic Park , died Monday in Sydney, Australia, at the age of 78.

articles.title: Third-party app stores coming to Google Play next week as Epic settlement withdrawn
articles.description: With the settlement withdrawn, Google is now bound by the court's full antitrust remedies.

articles.title: OpenAI's first branded hardware is... a light-up keyboard?
articles.description: The Codex Micro is designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance.

articles.title: A most improbable astronaut just went to space
articles.description: "I pretty much, at that point in time, gave up on being an astronaut."

articles.title: How hard is it to build orbital data centers, actually?
articles.description: "The ISS radiators are expensive and heavy. We're focused on making them cheap and light."

articles.title: Sotheby's big T. rex auction raises concerns hype and wealth are upending science
articles.description: Private buyers are increasingly outbidding museums for fossils.

articles.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
articles.description: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.

articles.title: Trump admin puts Americans in Congo on "do-not-board" list, barring return
articles.description: Citizens must now spend 21 days in a third country before they are allowed to come home.

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: Probe into explosive diarrheal cases points to Taco Bell and bad lettuce
articles.description: Federal officials have not confirmed a source yet—and there may be multiple sources.

articles.title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
articles.description: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.

articles.title: These painted e-tattoos could be the future of wearable biosensors
articles.description: Conductive ink is painted directly onto the skin in colorful custom designs, drying into working electrodes.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与数据隐私争议**：经期追踪应用Stardust被Mozilla研究指出与第三方分析公司共享用户健康数据，引发隐私担忧；Meta新增功能，当青少年与AI聊天机器人讨论自杀或自伤话题时，将通知家长。

2. **AI行业融资与投资动态**：前DeepMind研究员在未发布产品前即以3亿美元预种子轮估值融资；谢丽尔·桑德伯格领投1000万美元用于AI车辆检测服务；Ultrahuman前硬件副总裁获550万美元开发可控制AI代理的设备；Lululemon支持尼龙回收初创公司Syntetica完成3000万美元A轮融资。

3. **科技巨头战略调整**：OpenAI推出ChatGPT主题篮球引发市场好奇；苹果智能获准在中国与阿里巴巴、百度合作推出；一加手机宣布退出美国和欧洲市场；Greylock在可筹集更多资金的情况下将新基金规模上限设为15亿美元。

4. **黑客事件与安全**：英国警方称逮捕两名年轻黑客，成功扰乱了某臭名昭著黑客组织的运作。

5. **AI模型与行业应用**：月之暗面即将推出的Kimi 3预计将缩小与Anthropic Opus 4.8的差距；AMI Labs创始人拒绝将其AI称为“AGI”或“超级智能”；Applied Computing欲为油气工厂提供全厂AI模型。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：苹果智能正式获批在中国市场落地，并与阿里、百度合作，标志着中美科技巨头在AI领域合作的新里程碑。

articles.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception. This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what dri

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型安全与鲁棒性提升**：OpenAI发布“GPT-Red”项目，探索通过自我改进机制增强AI模型的鲁棒性；Hugging Face披露2026年7月安全事件，强调透明化处理与用户信任。
2. **AI应用与行业落地**：OpenAI多篇博客聚焦企业场景，包括销售团队与数据科学团队使用ChatGPT Work的实践，以及德国电信利用AI重构电信业务；Hugging Face分享构建智能体（如Shippy）的经验，并推出语音AI质量评估工具“Real World VoiceEQ”。
3. **技术前沿与工具优化**：Hugging Face探讨模型路由的复杂性，推出新项目“Inkling by Thinking Machines”；PyTorch发布性能分析系列第三篇，聚焦注意力机制优化；Google AI庆祝视觉搜索创新25周年。
4. **AI治理与政策推进**：OpenAI强调美国通过州与联邦层面行动推动AI安全发展，反映监管与产业协同趋势。
5. **入门与教育**：OpenAI发布ChatGPT使用指南，降低新用户门槛，推动AI工具普及。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI与Hugging Face在模型安全、企业应用及语音AI评估上的多维度突破，标志着AI正从技术验证加速迈向实际业务赋能与安全治理并重的新阶段。
